headline: AT&T reports Q1 2026 earnings with $31.5 billion in revenue, announces the closing of the Lumen fiber acquisition, and introduces a new segment reporting structure.
investor_impact: The realignment into Advanced Connectivity and Legacy segments offers investors clearer visibility into AT&T's core 5G and fiber growth thesis as copper revenues decline. The successful close of the Lumen acquisition and robust share repurchases signal strong capital execution, though a slight uptick in postpaid churn to 0.89% warrants monitoring.
key_figures: Q1 2026 operating revenues of $31.5 billion (up 2.9% YoY)
Q1 2026 income from continuing operations of $4.2 billion, or $0.54 per diluted share
Q1 2026 operating income of $6.7 billion with a 21.1% margin
Advanced Connectivity revenues of $28.5 billion (up 4.7% YoY)
158,000 retail wireless net adds, including 294,000 postpaid phone net adds
584,000 total internet connection net adds (292,000 fiber, 292,000 fixed wireless)
Legacy segment revenues of $1.8 billion (down 25.3% YoY)
Closed acquisition of Lumen's mass markets fiber business on February 2, 2026
$2.3 billion of common stock repurchased in Q1 2026, with $13.5 billion remaining under authorization
